title: About
intro: AI for Africa is an intensive two-week residential program bringing together up to 30 talented high school students for a hands-on introduction to artificial intelligence. Hosted on the African Leadership Academy (ALA) campus in Johannesburg, South Africa, the program is delivered in partnership with the Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI), one of the world's leading AI research institutions. It pairs ALA's deep expertise in leadership development with MBZUAI's academic strength in AI, giving participants the chance to learn foundational AI concepts, work alongside graduate-student mentors, and engage directly with world-class faculty. Together, the partners aim to equip the next generation of African innovators with the skills, confidence, and vision to shape the future of technology on the continent and beyond.

cards.title: MBZUAI and AI for sustainability
cards.body: MBZUAI frames artificial intelligence as a tool for solving real-world problems and advancing sustainable progress. Its research ecosystem spans areas that matter for public good, including agriculture, digital public health, and responsible AI applications.

cards.title: ALA and African society
cards.body: African Leadership Academy develops young leaders from across Africa and grounds the bootcamp in values of curiosity, integrity, service, and practical leadership for the communities participants come from.
